container.title: Workspace settings
container.name: Name
container.toolsHint: The ceiling for tools in this workspace; an agent can restrict further, never extend.
container.systemPromptHint: Replaces the default base instructions for chats in this workspace. Tool guidance (files, browser, memory) is still added on top. Agents keep their own.
container.systemPromptPlaceholder: e.g. Always answer concisely and cite file paths.

agents.title: Agents
agents.name: Name
agents.nameHelp: The agent's address: orchestrating agents run it by this name. Unnamed agents are invisible to them.
agents.descriptionHelp: The orchestrator-facing contract: what this agent does and what input it expects — it is all another agent sees when choosing.
agents.descriptionPlaceholder: Reviews a diff and reports findings by severity…
agents.systemHelp: Standing instructions — how the model should behave.
agents.userHelp: The default task for manual runs — seeded into the composer. Orchestrating agents supply their own task instead.
agents.workspaceHelp: On: runs bound to the active workspace, with its file tools and permissions. Off: a pure chat agent — never gets file access, runnable anywhere.
agents.needsWorkspace: This agent needs a workspace — select one in the sidebar to run it.
agents.permissionsHelp: This agent's ceiling. For each call the stricter of this and the workspace policy applies — an agent can restrict what the workspace grants, never extend it.
agents.permissionsWorkspace: This session runs "{{name}}" — the agent's limits apply on top of the workspace permissions.
agents.permissionsChat: "{{name}}" is a chat agent — workspace file tools are off in this session.
agents.permissionsDeleted: The linked agent no longer exists — plain workspace permissions apply.
agents.unlinkHint: The session keeps its transcript and system prompt; plain workspace / chat permissions apply from the next message.
agents.confirmUnlink: Unlink the agent from this session?
agents.deletedRunHint: This sub-agent run log was deleted. Its answer stays in the tool result.

account.title: Account
account.subtitle: Who you are, and how the harness connects.
account.offlineDesc: Standalone — local models, nothing leaves this machine.
account.connectedDesc: Link the company system — shared knowledge & sync.

provider.title: Provider
provider.subtitle: The model endpoint the chat and agents call.
provider.contextWindow: Context window: {{tokens}} tokens. Max output below is the per-response cap.

media.title: Media models
media.subtitle: Providers host the models that generate and understand media. Add providers and their models in the Providers tab, then pick one model per use case in Use cases.
media.coverageHint: What's covered and by which model — tools for uncovered use cases stay disabled.
media.noModels: no models available — add one in Providers
media.empty: No providers yet — add one to enable the media tools.
media.bareHint: Bare /generate endpoint — no model list, no model parameter. It has one default model: add it and tick what it does.
media.detectHint: Check the endpoint is reachable and list its models
media.customModelPlaceholder: model id (or Detect to list them)
media.promptStyleCosmos: Upsample prompts with the Cosmos JSON enhancer
media.electronOnly: Available in the desktop app only
media.poolHint: Each use case is a priority pool — the top model with free capacity is used first, spilling down.
media.concurrencyHint: Max concurrent in-flight calls on this model (default 5).
media.reserveHint: Slots kept for foreground only; the rest is lent to sub-agents (default 2).
media.ratingHint: Optional priority hint for ordering within a pool (higher first).

session.noImageSupport: This model doesn't accept images — enable image input in Settings → Provider to attach them.
session.noVideoSupport: This model doesn't accept video — enable video input in Settings → Provider to attach it.
session.disclaimer: Agents can make mistakes. Review their output.
session.contextFull: Context full — {{used}} / {{total}} tokens used. Start a new session to continue.
session.compacting: Summarizing earlier messages to free context…

progress.noModel: {{used}} used — detect a model to see the window size.
progress.summarizeBody: This compresses the entire conversation into a short summary and drops the full history (including attached images and files) to free up the context window. The model keeps the summary and continues from it. This can't be undone.

workspace.name: Name
workspace.defaultModelHint: Model new sessions in this workspace use.
workspace.isolationHint: A git worktree per session keeps parallel agents from clobbering files.
workspace.instructionsHint: Optional standing system prompt for agents in this workspace.
workspace.instructionsPlaceholder: e.g. This is a pnpm monorepo; run typecheck before finishing.
workspace.toolsHint: File tools are confined to this folder; destructive ones like Delete ask before acting.

storage.title: Storage
storage.subtitle: What your conversation history occupies, and where it lives. Delete sessions to free space.

developer.title: Developer
developer.subtitle: Diagnostics, logs, and feature flags.
developer.llmDebugHint: Logs every model request/response to the console (prefixed [llm]). On by default in dev.

system.title: Settings
system.subtitle: Your default instructions for the assistant in plain chats. Workspaces and agents can override it; tool guidance (files, browser, memory) is always added on top. Leave empty for the built-in default.
system.placeholder: e.g. You are my engineering assistant. Be concise, prefer code over prose, and always cite file paths.
system.languageHint: Use {{language}} anywhere — it's replaced with the user's current language.
system.defaultLabel: Built-in default (used when this is empty):
